kw.name: Create Dictionary
kw.doc: Creates and returns a dictionary based on the given ``items``.
kw.msg: &{createNewAccountBtn} = { en=Create new account | fr=Créer nouveau compte }

kw.name: Create Dictionary
kw.doc: Creates and returns a dictionary based on the given ``items``.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Evaluate
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Call Method
kw.doc: Calls the named method of the given object with the provided arguments.

kw.name: Call Method
kw.doc: Calls the named method of the given object with the provided arguments.

kw.name: Call Method
kw.doc: Calls the named method of the given object with the provided arguments.

kw.name: Call Method
kw.doc: Calls the named method of the given object with the provided arguments.

kw.name: Call Method
kw.doc: Calls the named method of the given object with the provided arguments.

kw.name: Create Webdriver
kw.doc: Creates an instance of Selenium WebDriver.
kw.msg: Creating an instance of the Chrome WebDriver.

kw.name: Go To
kw.doc: Navigates the current browser window to the provided ``url``.

kw.name: Set Window Size
kw.doc: Sets current windows size to given ``width`` and ``height``.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Enabled
kw.doc: Waits until the element ``locator`` is enabled.

kw.name: No Operation
kw.doc: Does absolutely nothing.

kw.name: Run Keyword Unless
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments if ``condition`` is false.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: No Operation
kw.doc: Does absolutely nothing.

kw.msg: Element '//ul[contains(@class, 'uiList localeSelectorList')]/li' not visible after 5 seconds.

test.status: Element '//ul[contains(@class, 'uiList localeSelectorList')]/li' not visible after 5 seconds.

suite.name: RestAPITests

test.name: GET an existing user, notice how the schema gets more accurate

kw.name: GET
kw.doc: *Sends a GET request to the endpoint.*

kw.name: Output Schema
kw.doc: *Outputs JSON Schema to terminal or a file.*

kw.name: Object
kw.doc: *Asserts the field as JSON object.*

kw.name: Integer
kw.doc: *Asserts the field as JSON integer.*

kw.name: String
kw.doc: *Asserts the field as JSON string.*

kw.name: String
kw.doc: *Asserts the field as JSON string.*

kw.name: Output Schema
kw.doc: *Outputs JSON Schema to terminal or a file.*

suite.doc: Test data can be read from variables and files. Both JSON and Python type systems are supported for inputs. Every request creates a so-called instance. Can be `Output`. Most keywords are effective only for the last instance. Initial schemas are autogenerated for request and response. You can make them more detailed by using assertion keywords. The assertion keywords correspond to the JSON types. They take in either path to the property or a JSONPath query. Using (enum) values in tests optional. Only type is required. All the JSON Schema validation keywords are also supported. Thus, there is no need to write any own validation logic. Not a long path from schemas to full Swagger/OpenAPI specs. The persistence of the created instances is the test suite. Use keyword `Rest instances` to output the created instances.
